sections.paragraph:
(for מַעַדְיָהּ “ornament of Jehovah”), [Maadiah], pr.n. m. Neh 12:5 for which there is מֹועַדְיָה (“festival of Jehovah”), verse 17 Neh 12:17. The two places will perfectly accord, if the first be read מֹעַדְיָה.

I. מַעֲדָן only in plur. מַ821ע֫֒דַנִּים, מַעֲדָנִים Jer 51:34 [In Thes. this reference is removed to עֵדֶן; The form actually occurring in the passage is מֵֽעֲדָנָי֑]; and מַעֲדַנֹּות 1Sa 15:32 (from the root עָדַן).

(1) delight, joy, Pro 29:17. Adv. with joy, joyfully, cheerfully, 1Sa 15:32.

(2) delicate food, dainties, Gen 49:20 Lam 4:5.

II. מַעֲדַנֹּות m. bonds; by transposition of the letters for מַעֲנַדֹּות, from the root עָנַד to bind. Job 38:31, מַעֲדַנֹּות כִּימָה “the bands of the Pleiades;” see כִּימָה .
